core_model.structure_summary: A planar autonomous first-order system in which each variable's rate is a nonlinear function of itself multiplied by (in the cross term) a nonlinear function of the other variable, with the coupling term entering the two equations with opposite signs, admitting an exactly conserved scalar function that renders the system canonically Hamiltonian after a monotone change of variables.
core_model.canonical_form: \dot{x}_1 = a\, f_1(x_1) f_2(x_2) - b\, f_1(x_1), \quad \dot{x}_2 = -c\, f_1(x_1) f_2(x_2) + d\, f_2(x_2); \qquad y_i = F_i(x_i),\ F_i' = 1/f_i \;\Rightarrow\; \dot{y}_1 = \partial H/\partial y_2,\ \dot{y}_2 = -\partial H/\partial y_1, \quad \dot{H} = 0

variables.meaning: two positive scalar quantities coupled antisymmetrically through a product of functions of each
four constant rate coefficients (two coupling strengths, two self-growth/decay rates)
the pair of monotone shape functions defining the family; specific choices recover the classical bilinear case or the exactly solvable case
conserved scalar function of both variables that generates the dynamics in transformed coordinates and confines orbits to its level curves

notes: The paper's contribution is the general Hamiltonian family of such antisymmetrically coupled product systems and the identification of the unique member with a closed-form elementary solution (transformed coupling function linear, i.e. quadratic inverse transform), plus power-law and N-component generalizations. The classical bilinear special case (f_i identity) is the cited background, not the core novelty.
